footnote: Titles count the whole English women's top flight — the FA Women's Premier League National Division (1992–2010) and the WSL (2011– ); the 2017 Spring Series doesn't count. Never-champions count from their first top-flight match. The 14 clubs shown are the 2026–27 season's, the league's first at that size.

lastTitle.clinch: won 4–0 at Brighton — Miedema, McCabe, Mead, van de Donk — to seal English title number 15 with a game to spare

teams.name: Aston Villa
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 0–4 at home to champions-to-be Croydon in their 1999 top-flight debut, then finished with one point from 18 games — six scored, 81 conceded — in one of English football's bleakest seasons.

teams.name: Birmingham City
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 1–1 with Leeds in their 2002 top-flight debut, a teenage Eni Aluko scoring; 2012 brought an FA Cup and a second straight runner-up finish — the title two agonizing points away.

teams.name: Brighton & Hove Albion
teams.firstGameNote: Beat Tranmere 1–0 in a long-forgotten 2001 National Division debut, were relegated by 2003, and returned via the WSL in 2018; their first FA Cup final, in 2026, ended 0–4 to Man City.

teams.name: Charlton Athletic

lastTitle.clinch: put six past Aston Villa on the season's final Saturday to retain the championship as Croydon — the club moved to Charlton that summer

teams.lineage: Bromley Borough (1991) → Croydon (1994–2000) → Charlton Athletic (2000– ); all three titles won as Croydon. Back in the top flight for 2026–27 after winning the first-ever WSL play-off.

lastTitle.clinch: won 1–0 at Manchester United through a Lucy Bronze header — a sixth straight title, clinched two games early in the WSL's first unbeaten season

teams.name: Crystal Palace
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 4–0 at Tottenham in their 2024 top-flight debut and went straight back down — then bounced right back, promotion sealed with a 6–1 final-day rout in May 2026.

lastTitle.clinch: confirmed champions without kicking a ball when Arsenal's midweek 3–3 draw with Croydon settled it — four days after Mo Marley's side ground out the decisive 0–0 at Croydon
beat Bristol Academy 3–0 on a wild final day, leaping from third to champions on goal difference as Chelsea fell at Man City

teams.name: London City Lionesses
teams.firstGameNote: Led Arsenal at the Emirates through an Asllani penalty in their 2025 debut before losing 4–1 — the WSL's first independent club, Michele Kang-owned, finished sixth in season one.

lastTitle.clinch: clinched without playing, when Arsenal's 1–1 draw at Brighton ended Chelsea's six-year reign and City's own ten-year wait

teams.name: Manchester United
teams.firstGameNote: Lost the first-ever WSL Manchester derby 1–0 at the Etihad before a record 31,213 in their 2019 debut; runners-up in 2023, FA Cup winners in 2024 — the league still missing.

teams.name: Tottenham Hotspur
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 1–0 at Stamford Bridge before nearly 25,000 in their 2019 WSL debut; two months later 38,262 watched them host Arsenal — the crowds arrived before the trophies.

teams.name: West Ham United
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 0–0 with Reading in their 2018 debut after leaping straight from the third tier on a license; they reached Wembley in year one, losing the 2019 FA Cup final.
